title: Research: do jmp condition-suffix mnemonics work? (Appendix B p.276 vs assembler error)
notes: Empirical research, no production change. Question: Appendix B p.276 says jmp takes the branch condition suffixes (jmpz/jmpn/...), but they error on assembly. Tested both tools: lccjs rejects jmpz/jmpn/jmpe with Invalid operation; oracle (cuh63 6.3) rejects jmpz IDENTICALLY — the leftover 7-byte .e is the deviation-10 blank artifact, not a real assembly (initial script misread the .e existence as acceptance; corrected). Static: assembler.js has no jmpXX cases, suffixes belong only to br; jmp encoding has no cc field. Conclusion: textbook DOC ERROR, not an lccjs gap — lccjs is parity-correct, no assembler fix. Recorded docs/research/jmp-condition-suffix-mnemonics.md, added a note to docs/lcc-isa.md, resolved the @todo #151 marker in happy-path.a. Note: happy-path.a exits 1 on a pre-existing brb error (line 43) on pristine main too — not caused here, out of scope. Branch needed a rebase onto apple #182/#183 before push (clean). Worktree (banana).

title: Test: linker aborts WITHOUT writing output on unresolved external (regression guard)
notes: Fourth child of the #171 spike (low — regression guard for the already-fixed OB-003/#33-35). 2 tests in linker.unit.spec.js: unresolved external -> link() throws LinkerError, createExecutable never called (pass-through spy), no out.e written in the mock fs; + a resolved-external contrast (link completes, createExecutable called once, out.e written) proving the abort is conditional not blanket. Confirms adjustExternalReferences runs before createExecutable in link(). Green on first run. 23/23 pass in spec. Marker /QA, logged TEST. Worked as apple alongside live banana (#151) + cherry (#160). actual ~1m.

title: Validation methodology: subagent prompts must say 'no file writes' explicitly
notes: Added a 'prompt discipline' note + reusable subagent-prompt template to docs/lccjs-assembly-skill-validation.md §Method: the #117 fan-out prompts fixed response format but not disk writes, so P2/N1 left stray .a files needing cleanup; the template now carries BOTH the format-only and the explicit 'do not write files to disk' constraint lines so future passes (§3.4/§3.5) leave a clean tree. Marked the parent validation-methodology TODOS bullet done. closed_commit is in lccjs (ships in-repo, not the cross-repo claude-config case). Worked as cherry alongside live banana/apple. actual ~7.5m (a couple min were Opus classifier outages, not work).

title: Test: linker createExecutable() byte format (signature/header/terminator + LE code words)
notes: Fifth/final child of the #171 spike — completes the epic. 2 tests in linker.unit.spec.js driving the previously-mocked createExecutable() through the virtualFs helper and asserting exact output bytes: o-signature, S+start(LE), G+addr+label+NUL, VTable-emitted-as-'A' entries BEFORE ATable 'A' entries (the spike's format refinement), C terminator, little-endian uint16 code; plus no 'S' without gotStart. Hand-computed byte buffers, green on first run. Full tests/new suite 600 passed/5 skipped, no regressions. Marker /QA, logged TEST. Worked as apple alongside live banana (#151) + cherry (#160). actual ~1.5m. #171 epic fully closed (#181-185).

title: Docs: capture multi-agent worktree concurrency failure modes (worktree-multi-agent-findings.md)
notes: Authored docs/worktree-multi-agent-findings.md: 7 enumerated failure modes from the 2026-05-29 multi-agent session (append-only CSV conflicts; rebase-then-push partial-state race; closed_commit rebase-orphaning; branch-protection notice; classifier outages; half-visible claim/marker state; transient worktree churn), root-caused to append-only-text-under-concurrent-writers, plus candidate mitigations. Left @todo #188 (open ARC spike) for the process decision; noted CHERRY's SQLite research as a candidate direction. Filed #188 (fix puzzle) + #189 (this doc task). Applied the just-documented lesson live: ran the rebase as a separate GATED step, not chained with push. Worked as apple. actual ~2m.

title: Research: CSV velocity log → merge=union + empty closed_commit (kill rebase-conflict churn)
notes: Spike for #186. Empirically verified in a throwaway repo that the built-in `union` merge driver FIRES UNDER git rebase (not just merge): two divergent branches each appending a CSV row auto-unioned with 0 conflict markers; same test confirmed the rebase still rewrites the commit SHA (79e238c->ca419d5), so merge=union fixes the manual-conflict pain (A) but NOT the closed_commit-orphan pain (B) — orthogonal. Recommendation (docs/research/velocity-log-storage.md): keep the CSV, add 'docs/puzzle-velocity.csv merge=union' to .gitattributes, switch to a single-commit close, log closed_commit EMPTY and derive via git log --grep 'Closes #N'. SQLite rejected (binary: unmergeable, undiffable, conflicts worse). Applied: .gitattributes line + docs/puzzle-velocity.md protocol/column rewrite + puzzle-velocity skill 0.6.0 in claude-config. THIS ROW dogfoods the new close: single commit, closed_commit intentionally empty. Follow-up: bulk closed_commit backfill reconciler. actual incl. collaborative scope review. Worked as cherry alongside live agents. [role reclassified RESEARCH->COMBO 2026-05-29 #192: original tag was RESEARCH; see role-table COMBO/SPIKE defs.]

title: Research/test: audit ALL branch-condition mnemonics for OG-LCC parity
notes: Test-first TDD audit. Parity spec over all 12 branch mnemonics: each assembles on lccjs + OG LCC to byte-identical .e, plus alias-pair encoding equality. GOTCHA: first cut used self-branch programs (main: brXX main) which infinite-loop the oracle (it RUNS what it assembles) -> spurious hangs + 5 false mismatches; fixed to forward-branch-to-halt. True result: ALL branch mnemonics at full byte-for-byte oracle parity EXCEPT brb (the #187 gap). Locked in as tests/new/assembler.branch-mnemonics.oracle.e2e.spec.js. Full suite 631 pass. Resolved #190 marker (assembler.js). Closed with #187 in b035a64. Worktree (banana).

notes: One-line fix surfaced by the #190 audit (TDD red): added the brb case to the branch dispatch in assembler.js (it was in assembleBR codes at cc=6 but never routed). brb now encodes 0x0c01 — byte-identical to the oracle and its brc alias. Unblocks demos/happy-path.a (was erroring at line 43). Verified by the #190 parity + alias-equality tests; @todo #187 marker in happy-path.a resolved. Closed with #190 in b035a64. title: Data: day-two re-run of the puzzle-velocity calibration analysis (notebook + new DATA role)
notes: Day-two re-run on the now-larger CSV (54-row calibration set vs ~36 day one). Duplicated stats/puzzle_velocity_analysis.ipynb -> stats/day-two-analysis.ipynb, same methodology for comparability, fully executed; all scope numbers recomputed live. NEW: §1 calendar-axis trend test (2 days now clear n>=5) + task-mix confound check; §7 auto-graduates per-role/test-loop hypotheses against the live power gate. Findings: bias STRONGER (51/54=94%% over-padded, sign-test p~1e-12); over-time axis now exists but raw rising trend (Spearman rho=+0.37,p=0.006) is most likely a task-MIX confound (day2 TEST-heavy; within-WRITER trend n.s.); per-role GRADUATED (TEST ~7.5x vs DEV ~2.6x; DEV-TEST p=0.008, TEST-WRITER p=0.022); LOC-null holds. New hypotheses: control for role before believing drift; TEST estimates want their own correction factor. Added DATA role code to docs/puzzle-velocity.md. Retroactive PDD puzzle (#191): no forward H/C estimate (ad-hoc analysis), wall-clock only; filed+closed same cycle. Refreshed stats/puzzle-velocity-enriched.csv via enrich.py. closed_commit empty per #186 single-commit protocol. Main session, no worktree (agent empty).
